CheckTitle: AI Agent Must Use DNSSEC/DoH/DoT for Secure DNS Resolution
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Network Security**. Verifies that every AI agent uses DNSSEC-validated resolvers, DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH), or DNS-over-TLS (DoT) for all DNS queries. Plaintext DNS is unauthenticated and unencrypted, making it trivially spoofable by network-level attackers.
Risk: An attacker with network access or who has compromised a DNS resolver can return forged DNS responses, redirecting agent API calls to attacker-controlled endpoints. Combined with TLS certificate bypass or a valid cert for the attacker's server, this constitutes a full MITM attack.

Code.Other:
1. Set uses_dnssec: true in the agent manifest.
2. Configure the agent's container or pod to use a DNSSEC-validating resolver (e.g., Unbound, systemd-resolved with DNSSEC=yes).
3. Alternatively, configure DoH or DoT using a resolver such as Cloudflare (1.1.1.1) or Google (8.8.8.8) with TLS pinning.

Recommendation.Text: Configure AI agents to use DNSSEC-validating resolvers or encrypted DNS (DoH/DoT) for all name resolution. Prevent fallback to plaintext DNS using resolver policy configuration.

Notes: ASPM-045 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
